description: How TOEFL questions map to passage content — paraphrase strategies, distractor engineering, vocabulary patterns

synonym_paraphrase.note: Most common — replaces key words with synonyms

inference_synthesis.note: Combines info from 1-2 sentences, draws unstated conclusion

near_verbatim_restatement.note: Close to passage wording, minor syntax changes

factual_detail.note: About 4 in 7 content words shared with passage

inference.note: Much lower — answer says what passage implies

rhetorical_purpose.note: Uses meta-language ('to illustrate', 'to provide evidence') not in passage

correctAnswerMapping.keyRule: Correct answers NEVER use a phrase longer than 3 consecutive passage words. Always restructured syntactically.

not_mentioned.note: Introduces topically-related but fabricated content. Borrows ZERO passage vocabulary.

wrong_detail.note: MOST DANGEROUS. Reuses passage vocabulary but changes relationships. Avg 2-3 passage content words.

opposite.note: Reverses passage logic. E.g., 'less intelligent' → 'more intelligent'

plausible_but_unsupported.note: Sounds reasonable from world knowledge but has no textual basis

inference.note: Opposite is dominant — tests logical direction

main_idea.note: Distractors capture only one detail, not the whole scope

negative_factual.note: 3 options ARE true (paraphrased from passage), 1 is NOT

rhetorical_purpose.note: Wrong purpose for why author mentions something

vocabulary_in_context.note: Options are plausible meanings but wrong for THIS context

vocabularyBorrowing.distractorsWithPassageWords: 40% of all distractors contain ≥1 passage content word
vocabularyBorrowing.note: wrong_detail type borrows most heavily; not_mentioned type borrows least

lengthRelationship.note: Distractors tend to be SHORTER than correct answers (50% vs 17% vs 32%)

optionsWithRealDefinitions.note: Usually only 1 option is a real definition; others are same-POS words

vocabularyQuestionProfile.contextCluePattern: Passage almost always has an appositive or explanatory clause adjacent to the tested word
vocabularyQuestionProfile.distractorConstruction: Options are real English words of same POS that could superficially fit the sentence but not the semantic context

questionOrdering.rule: Questions generally follow passage order but with exceptions for span-questions (negative_factual, main_idea, paragraph_relationship)
